words.hint: The app where you scroll short videos and everyone does the same dances
The photo and video app with Stories, Reels, and the grid aesthetic
The app where photos disappear after viewing and you keep streaks
The app that forces you to take a simultaneous front and back camera photo daily
The server-based chat app where gaming communities and friend groups live
Your private secondary Instagram account for your real friends
Your main curated Instagram account that strangers can see
The invisible system that decides what content shows up in your feed
TikTok's For You Page where videos from strangers appear
When your post gets millions of views seemingly overnight
A secret second account where you post without worrying about your image
A photo or video that disappears after 24 hours
Instagram's short-form vertical video feature
When a reply gets more likes than the original post — brutal
When a platform limits your reach without telling you why
When the internet collectively decides to stop supporting someone for something they did
A video diary of your life posted to YouTube
Point of View — a TikTok format where you act out a scenario
A trend where creators act like video game characters who repeat scripts for tips
The state of being so deep in internet culture that normal things feel weird
Being so terminally online that you lose touch with real-world norms
Posting a very attractive photo of yourself to get compliments
Streaming to followers in real time on Instagram or TikTok
Posting about someone without naming them but everyone knows who it's about
Making it so a specific person can no longer see your profile or contact you
Removing someone from your followed accounts — usually dramatic
People on social media who follow each other
Following someone's account, often after seeing their content
A cohesive visual vibe — dark academia, cottagecore, Y2K, etc.
A suffix for an internet aesthetic trend — cottagecore, normcore, fairycore
You did something amazing and you know it
When someone perfectly executed what was expected — usually in fashion or looks
A phrase to describe the energy or vibe something exudes — 'it's giving main character'
An insult telling someone to go outside and reconnect with the real world
For real, I'm not lying — used to emphasize truth
A lie — 'that's cap' means you're calling someone out for lying
Secretly or subtly — 'I lowkey love this song'
Very much or obviously — 'I highkey want this'
Something is really good — usually used for food
Suspicious — popularized by Among Us
When something lives in your head and you can't stop thinking about it
Short for delusional — when you're hoping for something completely unrealistic
Wildly out of control in an entertaining way
Holding your own opinion without caring what others think — a compliment
An assessment of someone's energy or mood
Acting like you're the center of the universe and the story is about you
Someone who seems like they exist only as a background person in someone else's story
Said at the end of a statement to indicate it's final and not up for debate
Okay, agreed, or sounds good — usually as a one-word response
When something affects you more than expected, usually emotionally or contextually
Average, mediocre, nothing special
Excellent, impressive — the highest compliment
